segments.text: In the year that King Uzziah died, I saw the Lord sitting on a throne, high and lifted up; and his train filled the temple.
Above him stood the seraphim. Each one had six wings. With two he covered his face. With two he covered his feet. With two he flew.
One called to another, and said,
Holy, holy, holy, is Yahweh of Armies! The whole earth is full of his glory!
segments.speaker: Seraphim

segments.text: The foundations of the thresholds shook at the voice of him who called, and the house was filled with smoke.
Then I said,
Woe is me! For I am undone, because I am a man of unclean lips and I live among a people of unclean lips, for my eyes have seen the King, Yahweh of Armies!
segments.speaker: One

segments.text: Then one of the seraphim flew to me, having a live coal in his hand, which he had taken with the tongs from off the altar.
He touched my mouth with it, and said,
Behold, this has touched your lips; and your iniquity is taken away, and your sin forgiven.
segments.speaker: Seraphim

segments.text: I heard the Lord’s voice, saying,
Whom shall I send, and who will go for us?
segments.speaker: God

segments.text: Then I said,
Here I am. Send me!
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: He said,
Go, and tell this people,‘You hear indeed, but don’t understand. You see indeed, but don’t perceive.’
segments.speaker: God

segments.text:
Make the heart of this people fat. Make their ears heavy, and shut their eyes; lest they see with their eyes, hear with their ears, 
understand with their heart, 
and turn again, and be healed.
segments.speaker: God

segments.text: Then I said,
Lord, how long?
segments.speaker: Isaiah

segments.text: He answered,

Until cities are waste without inhabitant, houses without man, 
the land becomes utterly waste,
segments.speaker: God

segments.text:
and Yahweh has removed men far away, 
and the forsaken places are many within the land.
segments.speaker: The LORD

segments.text: If there is a tenth left in it, that also will in turn be consumed, as a terebinth, and as an oak whose stump remains when they are cut down, so the holy seed is its stump.
segments.speaker: God
